heading: Treatment of accounts
label: 763 Treatment of accounts
notes: senate report no. 95–989Section 763 provides for separate treatment of accounts held in separate capacities. A deficit in one account held for a customer may not be offset against the net equity in another account held by the same customer in a separate capacity or held by another customer.
Amendments1984—Subsec. (a). substituted “by the debtor for” for “by” and “treated as” for “deemed to be”.Pub. L. 98–353

Effective Date of 1984 AmendmentAmendment by effective with respect to cases filed 90 days after , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 98–353section 552(a) of Pub. L. 98–353section 101 of this titleJuly 10, 1984
amendments: Amendments1984—Subsec. (a). substituted “by the debtor for” for “by” and “treated as” for “deemed to be”.Pub. L. 98–353

Effective Date of 1984 AmendmentAmendment by effective with respect to cases filed 90 days after , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 98–353section 552(a) of Pub. L. 98–353section 101 of this titleJuly 10, 1984

children.label: a
children.text: Accounts held by the debtor for a particular customer in separate capacities shall be treated as accounts of separate customers.

children.label: b
children.text: A member of a clearing organization shall be deemed to hold such member’s proprietary account in a separate capacity from such member’s customers’ account.

children.label: c
children.text: The net equity in a customer’s account may not be offset against the net equity in the account of any other customer.
